basics.name: Mahd Mahmud
basics.label: Ph.D. Candidate in Economics
basics.summary: Ph.D. Candidate in Economics at Michigan State University. Research interests: Household Finance, Mortgage Markets, Housing and Consumer Credit, Financial Crises and Policy Design, Distributional Effects of Macroeconomic Policy.

work.name: Federal Reserve Board of Governors
work.position: Senior Research Assistant, Division of Research & Statistics
highlights: Conducted empirical research on mortgage markets, government-sponsored enterprises (GSEs), and household balance sheets.
Built and analyzed large loan-level datasets using administrative mortgage performance data.
Produced figures, tables, and statistical analyses for internal research projects and policy briefings.
Collaborated with economists on projects related to financial stability and consumer credit.
Participated in the Howard University–Federal Reserve Board Economics Program (ECON 181); worked as TA, project buddy and course coordinator.
Fields: Macroeconomics, Household Finance, Housing Economics

publications.name: Housing Market Stability during Crises: Efficacy of Refinancing and Forbearance in a Heterogeneous-Agent Model

skills.name: Data Experience
keywords: Loan-level mortgage data (public and private sources)
Household survey data (SCF, PSID, ATUS, CEX)
